analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$76,000-76,340 across exchanges as of Apr 30 12:17 UTC. Down from weekly high ~$79K. Twice rejected at $80K resistance. Support ~$74,959 (24h low). Progressively lower opening prices each morning this week.
sentiment_key_signals: FOMC COMPLETED Apr 29: held 3.5-3.75%; unusual 8-4 dissent — most divided vote since Oct 1992
Still within 48h post-FOMC window — historical BTC weakness pattern (8 of last 9 meetings) still active
BTC twice rejected at $80K resistance — technical ceiling firm (double top pattern)
Spot BTC ETF outflows: $263.2M (Apr 27) + $89.7M (Apr 28) — two confirmed consecutive outflow days
BTC opening prices declining every morning this week — sustained sell-side pressure
Brent crude >$104/barrel (Hormuz restrictions) — sustained inflation headwind
GDP Q1 advance + PCE due TODAY Apr 30 12:30 UTC — Atlanta Fed nowcast ~1.2% vs consensus 2.5-3%
NFP May 1 12:30 UTC — directional impact depends on print
Geopolitical tensions (U.S.-Iran, Strait of Hormuz) weighing on risk assets
Fear & Greed Index recovering from lows but fragile; negative funding rates signal trader skepticism
analysis.macro_backdrop: Post-FOMC: Fed held at 3.5-3.75% with 8-4 dissent (most divided since Oct 1992). Oil >$104 (Hormuz) keeps inflation elevated. GDP Q1 advance + PCE releasing TODAY at 12:30 UTC — stagflation risk high if GDP misses and PCE beats. Two consecutive days of ETF outflows and $80K double top reinforce bearish short-term bias. NFP on May 1 is next major binary catalyst.
analysis.regime_rationale: Bearish (0.62): FOMC bearish 48h pattern still active, $80K double rejection, consecutive ETF outflows, declining weekly open prices, Hormuz oil headwinds, weak GDP nowcast, geopolitical tensions. Confidence 0.62 (>=0.5 threshold) — macro filter ACTIVE. Prefer SHORT bias or cautious HOLD. Reassess after GDP/PCE release at 12:30 UTC.
